programmes.show_description: Charlie, who's 65, is back for the second time in two weeks after previously being discharged following a crash. And Peggy, who's 89, is rushed in with a suspected stroke.
The paramedics help a man who's collapsed and may be having a heart attack and an elderly woman who has climbed on a chair to reach a daddy long-legs, with painful consequences.
Motorcyclist Brendan has had a head-on collision with a car. Teenager Alex has badly injured his hand punching a window. And the resus team struggle to revive two patients.
Charlie, who's 65, is back for the second time in two weeks after previously being discharged following a crash. And Peggy, who's 89, is rushed in with a suspected stroke.
...Veg Aisle: The team get the inside info on summer veggies in winter, temperamental bananas, and how to waste less produce. (Ep3/4)

Continuing their tour of the Scottish Highlands, the campers soak up some history and feed the deer on the 145,000-acre Atholl Estate at Blair Castle Caravan Park.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon O'Brien repurposes some old suitcases, while Henry Cole gives a dated dresser some serious bling.
Carrie and Steve are looking for their dream property in the town of Islantilla on Spain's Costa de la Luz. Can Leah Charles-King find their perfect place for their £110,000 budget?
Sheffield. Flamboyant performing arts teacher Helen's food doesn't go to plan, but she delivers an entertaining night featuring trumping, spies, and a dessert that goes with a bang. (Ep1/5)
Sheffield. Design and technology teacher Claire serves up an Asian menu, on a night that features spicy food, some plasticine modelling and a sprinkling of offensive comments... (Ep2/5)
Sheffield. Service administrator Monika hosts a traditional Bulgarian night inspired by her homeland. But Simon's sexist jokes leave a bitter taste. (Ep3/5)
Sheffield. Musician Ivor plans an 80s extravaganza, with a live performance from his band and a jazzed-up menu including 'curiosity killed the coq au vin'. (Ep4/5)
Sheffield. Former chef Simon is confident that his experimental gastro cuisine will impress. But can he contend with the tension among his guests? (Ep5/5)
Heather and Sally hope that their fussy cleaning regime will pay off at Brockley Hall Hotel. But the Yorkshire breakfast puzzles the guests and some of the feedback is hard to swallow.
Alistair, who's fitted out luxury hotels including The Savoy, hosts at his refurbished village pub, The Star Inn 1744, in Leicestershire. But his breakfast ordering system gets a grilling.
Michelle and Jason are keen to show off their luxury lodges at Croft Farm Park in Gloucestershire. The group go canoeing, but some struggle to stay afloat when one guest sticks his oar in.
Steve and Tracy welcome their rivals to their homely B&B, Harbour Vista, in Poole. But most of the guests are surprised that it's not more lavish...
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for the last time to settle some scores. Accusations fly as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Volvo P1800: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend save a 1964 Volvo P1800S, synonymous with 60s TV series The Saint, that's been rusting away in a Croydon garage for 30 years.
Singer Le Mans: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend fix up a 1934 Singer Le Mans two-seater sports car that had its last outing at a wedding in 1998 and has been in a barn ever since.
Pocket rocket Penny's just right for Josan, but there's someone else she needs to impress... And can tiny 10-week-old Ember handle Staffy Bronson's rough play?
Brand new series - VW Polo G40: Tim and Fuzz celebrate the VW Polo's 50th birthday by fixing up Anthony's rare 1991 G40. And from Germany, the story behind this supercharged spec.
...Penelope Keith: Brand new series - In Chillingham, Britain's most haunted castle reveals its secrets. The owners of Iford Manor buy a pub. And Whitmore Hall has a spring clean. (Ep8)
Patients on the nightshift include a stabbing victim, an 86-year-old woman who's spent her life alone, and a former lawyer who's lost his wife, family and home to alcoholism.
The medical team, patients and their families face the meaning of life and death. One patient, 77-year-old John, faces a dangerous operation for a potentially deadly aneurysm.
...Penelope Keith: Brand new series - In Chillingham, Britain's most haunted castle reveals its secrets. The owners of Iford Manor buy a pub. And Whitmore Hall has a spring clean. (Ep8)
Patients on the nightshift include a stabbing victim, an 86-year-old woman who's spent her life alone, and a former lawyer who's lost his wife, family and home to alcoholism.
The medical team, patients and their families face the meaning of life and death. One patient, 77-year-old John, faces a dangerous operation for a potentially deadly aneurysm.
...Snacks Aisle: How many mangoes are in a dried mango pack? The team also crack the secret of caramel popcorn and the mystery of snowballs' fluffy centre. (Ep4/4)

The campers pitch up at at the foot of Scotland's most famous mountain at Ben Nevis Holiday Park, where they take aim at a five-star rating with axe throwing and mountain biking.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien upcycle some stained glass and a Victorian hay rack, to try to turn a profit for their owners.
Leah Charles-King is in Mijas Costa helping Susan and Terry to find their forever home. They've given Leah a budget of £120,000 to find their perfect two-bedroom property near the beach.
County Durham. Hairdresser Julie plans a no-frills menu that she can prepare in advance. There's a tangle at the table when David makes a nasty comment about Stephen's dreadlocks. (Ep1/5)
County Durham. Beauty school teacher Ashleigh serves up sophisticated grub with a side order of hearts and love. (Ep2/5)
County Durham. Part-time farmer Stephen invites his guests to an evening on his farm, complete with a drinks reception in the stables and rustic home-cooked cuisine. (Ep3/5)
County Durham. Prison transport officer Tina's sophisticated French menu includes salmon crêpes and posh peas, with expectations high after her criticisms earlier in the week. (Ep4/5)
County Durham. IT manager David hosts a French evening, but can he keep Ashleigh and Tina away from each other's throats? (Ep5/5)
The first hosts, Ronnie and Steph, welcome their rivals to the Inn at the Park in Aberdeen, where one guest's culinary background raises the stakes at breakfast.
Shauna and Fiona host at Ingliston Country Club in Renfrewshire, where dated décor and a delayed breakfast lead to feedback that causes a storm in a teacup.
Steve and Allard host at the B&B they built themselves, 1692 Wasdale, in the Lake District. Shauna and Fiona think it's picture-perfect, but John has concerns about cleanliness.
The final visit is to the Lynbar Hotel, where confident John boasts that he serves the best breakfast in Blackpool. But do his guests agree?
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for the last time to find out how much they've been paid and to settle some scores, before the winner is announced.
Mazda MX5: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue a 25-year-old example of the world's bestselling two-seater sports car: the Mazda MX5. But it's been off the road for some time.
Range Rover Vogue SE: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Cambridge to rescue a cherished but tired and rusty 1991 Range Rover Vogue SE that needs some TLC.
The team at picturesque doggy sanctuary Animal Welfare League NSW hope that neo-mastiff cross Goober's drool won't be too much for Scott and Adelaide.
Furniture maker Lucinda wants to construct a beautifully crafted, two-storey, highly-insulated timber home in woodland in South Herefordshire... without using heavy machinery.
Duncan and Liz build a revolutionary Passivhaus Premium powerhouse of a home in the Cotswolds. But with a spiralling schedule and lonesome living, will it all be worth it in the end?
There's a variety of sport and drink-related injuries on the Saturday shift. And when a family party goes horribly wrong, a dad and son sustain life-threatening injuries.
